how: For each page: use LLM to generate a 3-5 sentence summary capturing: (1) core topic, (2) key claims/facts, (3) target audience, (4) unique value proposition. Review and refine the summary manually. Use this summary as the input for intent inference, not the raw page content.
expected_impact: 显著提升下游意图推断和内容优化的质量
how_to_verify: 对比有无summarization步骤的最终内容优化质量（人工评估+Probe指标）
_title: Role-Augmented Intent-Driven Generative Search Engine Optimization (RAID G-SEO)

name: Step Planning for Controlled Content Rewriting
what: Before rewriting, generate an explicit, interpretable optimization plan listing specific revision steps. Use this plan to guide the rewriting process, preventing semantic drift. Each step should be a concrete action (e.g., 'add comparison table', 'restructure FAQ with question-based headings').
why: Uncontrolled LLM rewriting often introduces semantic drift — the rewritten content loses alignment with the original meaning. Step planning decomposes the optimization intent into actionable revisions, enabling controllability and ensuring the rewritten content preserves the core semantic focus.
how: For each page: (1) based on refined intent, generate 5-10 specific optimization steps; (2) review and prioritize steps; (3) execute each step sequentially using LLM with explicit instructions; (4) verify each step doesn't alter original meaning. Document the step plan for reproducibility and iteration.
expected_impact: 语义漂移控制——优化后内容保持与原始内容的核心一致性
how_to_verify: 人工审查：对比优化前后的核心信息是否有损失；Probe忠实度评分
_title: Role-Augmented Intent-Driven Generative Search Engine Optimization (RAID G-SEO)

name: Listwise Prompting for Content Ranking Optimization
what: Use Listwise prompting with LLMs to optimize content for ranking: provide the LLM with past ranking lists (query + ranked documents from highest to lowest) as context. Ask the LLM to modify a target document to improve its ranking, using the ranking patterns as implicit training data.
why: Listwise bots achieved 0.315-0.341 Scaled Promotion vs baseline 0.309, significantly outperforming human students (-0.09 to -0.128). By showing the LLM complete ranked lists, it learns the implicit ranking function patterns — which content features correlate with high vs low rankings.
how: For each target page: (1) collect ranking data for your query cluster (top-10 results for related queries). (2) Construct a Listwise prompt: 'query: [your target query]; documents ordered by ranking from highest to lowest: [list top 5 with their content].' (3) Prompt LLM: 'Edit the candidate document to improve its ranking, using the past rankings as guidance.' (4) Apply modifications that maintain content faithfulness.
expected_impact: 排名提升+0.315到+0.341 Scaled Promotion（显著优于人类学生）
how_to_verify: 对比优化前后的搜索排名变化；Probe追踪AI引擎引用位置变化
_title: White Hat Search Engine Optimization using Large Language Models

name: Pairwise Few-Shot Learning from Competitor Rankings
what: Use Pairwise prompting: provide the LLM with query + pairs of documents and their relative rankings (doc A ranked higher than doc B). This helps the LLM learn what distinguishes high-ranked from low-ranked content. Use multiple pairs from recent rankings.
why: Pairwise bots achieved the highest Scaled Promotion (0.345) outperforming all other methods and baselines. The paired comparison format helps LLMs identify specific features that differentiate winning content from losing content, making it more actionable than pointwise or listwise approaches.
how:
For your target query: (1) collect 3 highest-ranked competitor pages and 3 lower-ranked pages. (2) Create prompt: 'query: [query]
document: [high-ranked content], latest ranking: #1
document: [low-ranked content], latest ranking: #8'. (3) Include 2-3 such pairs from recent ranking rounds. (4) Prompt LLM to modify your document using these patterns. Set temperature=0.5 for balanced creativity.
expected_impact: 排名提升+0.345 Scaled Promotion（最佳方法）
how_to_verify: A/B对比：Pairwise优化后内容vs原始内容的Probe排名
_title: White Hat Search Engine Optimization using Large Language Models

name: Faithfulness-Constrained Content Modification
what: When using LLMs to optimize content for ranking, explicitly constrain modifications to maintain faithfulness to the original document. Target the most impactful sentences for editing, not full rewrites. Keep modified content within the target length range of competitor documents.
why: The paper's general prompt explicitly directs the LLM to 'ensure the edited document is very similar to the candidate document' and to 'focus on editing the most impactful sentences.' This constraint is critical — unconstrained LLM rewrites can drift semantically (as shown in SAGEO Arena paper 034) and lose ranking-relevant content.
how: Modify your content optimization prompt to include: 'Edit the document to improve its ranking. Focus on editing the most impactful sentences. Target an edited document length of around [median competitor length] words. Ensure the edited document is very similar to the original. Generate only the edited document.' Apply NLI-based faithfulness checking (TrueTeacher) to verify modifications don't alter core meaning.
expected_impact: 保持高保真度(OrigFaith 0.57-0.84)的同时实现排名提升
how_to_verify: 使用NLI模型(TrueTeacher)检测优化后内容的保真度；确保OrigFaith >= 0.7
_title: White Hat Search Engine Optimization using Large Language Models

name: Competitive Ranking Context Injection for Content Strategy
what: Regularly collect and analyze ranking data for your target queries. Feed this competitive intelligence into your content optimization process: what are top-ranked pages doing that you're not? Use this data as context for LLM-guided optimization, not just manual analysis.
why: All effective methods in this paper rely on past ranking data as context. The LLM implicitly learns the undisclosed ranking function from examples. Without competitive ranking data, content optimization is guesswork. With it, the LLM can identify and replicate winning patterns while avoiding losing patterns.
how: Set up monthly competitive ranking snapshots: (1) for top-10 target queries, capture SERP results and content of top-5 pages; (2) identify patterns: common structural elements, content depth, keyword usage, authority signals; (3) feed this into Listwise or Pairwise prompts for LLM-guided content updates; (4) track ranking changes post-optimization.
expected_impact: 基于数据的排名优化，显著优于无竞争情报的零样本改写
how_to_verify: 建立排名追踪表，按月对比优化前后排名变化
_title: White Hat Search Engine Optimization using Large Language Models
